content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
PO BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

February 1, 1978

Robert P. Will
General Counsel
The Metropolitan Water District
of Southern California
Post Office Box 54151
Los Angeles, California 90054

Dear Mr. Will:

This is in response to your letter to Mr. [illegible] dated January 4,
1978, requesting that certain amendments to the Conflict of Interest Code
of the District be deemed non-substantive in nature pursuant to 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18752.

As outlined in your letter, it appears that the amendments in question
fit within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752(d)(1) and (4).
Accordingly, they are hereby deemed non-substantive and may be made on
an interim basis subject to formal approval of the Commission not later
than April 1979.

I would point out that to the extent the amendments fit within sub-
section (d)(4) "the person occupying a position designated pursuant to
this subsection may within 10 working days of notification of designation
file objection with the filing officer of the agency. The filing of an
objection shall render the proposed designation ineffective until the
provisions of Government Code Section 87311 are satisfied with respect
to the designation of the position."

I would also stress that the designation of these amendments as non-
substantive has no effect on the proposed amendments to the Metropolitan
Water District Code outlined in your letter to the Commission dated January 25.
A member of the staff will be in contact with your office in the near
future concerning those matters.

Thank you for writing.

Sincerely,

Kenneth W. Gosborn
Research Specialist III
Conflict of Interest Division

The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

January 4, 1973


Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Request to Categorize Conflict of Interest
Code Amendment as Nonsubstantive

The Metropolitan Water District has recently est-
ablished three occupational titles and has filled one previously
vacant occupational title. As a result it appears necessary to
make changes in the District's Conflict of Interest and Disclosure
of Personal Finances Code. Also, one position previously classi-
fied as a "designated employee" has been eliminated. The occupa-
tional titles and the changes which appear to be necessary are
as follows:

"Assistant Director of Personnel" and "Principal
Personnel Analyst" should be included in the same classification
as Director of Personnel and Senior Personnel Analyst in Section V
of Appendix A.

"Material Control Coordinator" should be included in
the same classification as Superintendent of Stores and Princi-
pal Clerk-Stores in Section II of Appendix A.

The new title of "Operations Services Manager" should
replace that of Principal Engineer-Technician in Section III B
of Appendix A.

Mr. Michael Bennett                -2-                January 4, 1978


     Pursuant to 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18752, request
is hereby made of the Executive Director of the Fair Political
Practices Commission to classify the above as nonsubstantive.

     If approval is received, this office will recommend
to the District's Board of Directors that the changes be made.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert P. Will
                                        General Counsel
